content.body:
Ingestion is currently one-shot — triggered manually via POST /ingest. For beta and future analytics to be useful, data must stay current.

**Needed:**
- Periodic ingestion trigger (daily at minimum, hourly preferred)
- Could be systemd timer, cron job, or internal scheduler (apalis is already a dependency)
- Must handle failures gracefully (retry, alert on consecutive failures)
- Ingestion status should be queryable (GET /ingestion/status already exists)

Without this, beta values will go stale immediately after deployment.
content.title: Scheduled data ingestion (cron)

content.body:
POST /beta returns a beta value without indicating whether the underlying data is stale. If ingestion hasn't run in days, the beta is computed from old prices.

**Needed:**
- Check the most recent candle timestamp before computing beta
- Return staleness info alongside the beta value (e.g. `{ beta: 0.59, data_age_hours: 2 }`)
- Frontend should warn if data is older than a threshold (e.g. 24h)

content.body:
Currently, deploying non-master branches requires editing the CI deploy gate each time (adding branch names to the if condition). This is error-prone and wastes time.

**Needed:**
- A staging environment (separate DigitalOcean droplet or same host with different port/config)
- CI deploys to staging on pushes to designated branches (e.g. any PR branch, or a staging branch)
- CI deploys to production only on master
- Staging should use the same NixOS config but with a different hostname/IP

This unblocks testing deployment changes, backend features, and infrastructure without touching production.
content.title: Add staging environment for pre-production deployments

content.body:
When attempting to fully close the portfolio (set all target weights to 0%), the frontend validation blocks the rebalance with the error:

> Sum of weights is below 100% by 100.0%. Add allocations.

This makes it impossible to use the current portfolio UI to fully exit all positions into cash.

## Expected behavior

- User can set all portfolio weights to 0% and submit a rebalance.
- For all existing positions, the system emits full-close orders (reduce positions to zero).
- The total-weights validation does not block this specific \"close all\" scenario.

## Actual behavior

- When all weights are set to 0%, the validation message appears:
  \`Sum of weights is below 100% by 100.0%. Add allocations.\`
- The Rebalance button remains disabled, so a full portfolio close is not possible.

## Notes

- The \"sum of weights must be ~100%\" rule is useful for normal rebalance flows, but it should allow an exception when:
  - all target weights are 0%, and
  - there is at least one existing position in the initial portfolio (we are closing, not reallocating).
- The staged changes logic already builds proper delete/close operations for existing positions; the blocker is only the high-level validation in \`usePortfolioState\` (blockingReasons/disableSubmit)."
content.title: Cannot fully close portfolio due to 100% weights validation

milestone.description: Users need to reach the app before any portfolio feature matters. Deployment, domain, smoke-tested rebalancing.
milestone.title: Usable production deployment

items.title: Cannot fully close portfolio due to 100% weights validation

content.body:
Right now the frontend’s notion of the portfolio state is not reliably in sync with what actually happens on Hyperliquid after a rebalance.

What currently happens:

- We construct a rebalance payload that includes:
  - modified positions (e.g. BTC from current notional to target notional),
  - `deleted` positions (full closes, e.g. APE with percentage → 0 and `status: "deleted"`).
- The Hyperliquid client:
  - processes `deleted` positions first via `closePosition` (reduce-only market close),
  - then rebalances the rest (`processPosition` / `placeOrder`), logging a purple `[Rebalance] Deletions:` table and a purple `[Rebalance] Order preview:` table.
- On `onSuccess` of the mutation we currently:
  - assume the orders “logically” went through,
  - trigger a refetch of positions and account summary (with a small polling window), and
  - rebuild the portfolio state and staged changes from those refetched positions.

The problem:

- In practice, it’s very easy for the UI to show a “completed” rebalance while the actual Hyperliquid positions remain unchanged or only partially updated:
  - deleted positions can still be present after a “successful” rebalance (e.g. APE remains open even though it was marked `deleted` and logged in `[Rebalance] Deletions`),
  - modified positions can keep their old notional (e.g. BTC stays near the old size even though the order preview clearly shows a large `deltaNotional`).
- Our current logic treats the HTTP/ccxt call success + a timed refetch as if it were proof that “the portfolio is now in the target state”, which is not always true:
  - orders can be rejected, partially filled, or still in-flight on the exchange side,
  - ccxt fetches are snapshot-based and may race with order processing,
  - we don’t have a strong source of truth that “all relevant orders have finished and the on-chain / exchange state is settled”.

Why this is dangerous:

- The UI can present a “green” state (no staged changes, rebalance button idle) while the actual positions on Hyperliquid are still in the *previous* configuration or some inconsistent intermediate state.
- For a hedging / risk-management tool this is particularly bad: the user may think they are hedged or fully closed when they are not.
- Staged changes semantics become unreliable, because we clear or re-base them based on an assumption of sync rather than confirmed execution.

What we need instead:

- A robust way to know when orders are truly done and the positions snapshot is trustworthy. Concretely:
  - Use Hyperliquid’s websocket (or another streaming mechanism, if available) to subscribe to:
    - order lifecycle (submitted / open / partially filled / filled / canceled / rejected),
    - position updates for the relevant symbols.
  - Track **per-rebalance** the set of symbols we touched (from the payload) and wait until:
    - all related orders for those symbols reach a terminal state (filled / canceled / rejected), and
    - the positions stream shows a new stable state for those symbols (no further updates for some short window).
- Only after this “convergence” should we:
  - rebuild `initialPortfolio` / `selectedTokens` from the latest positions,
  - clear staged changes for the successfully converged symbols,
  - flip the UI out of the “Rebalancing” / “pending” state.

Acceptance criteria:

- When I rebalance:
  - deleting a position (e.g. APE) and resizing another (e.g. BTC) results in the on-screen portfolio matching **exactly** what Hyperliquid reports, without ghost positions or stale notionals.
- The UI never shows “no staged changes” / “rebalance complete” while:
  - a position that was supposed to be deleted is still open on Hyperliquid, or
  - a position that was supposed to be resized is still at (approximately) its old notional.
- The implementation should be based on a streaming / event-driven confirmation (websocket or equivalent), not just timed HTTP refetch plus optimistic assumptions.
content.title: Portfolio state is not reliably synced with actual Hyperliquid positions

milestone.description: Users need to reach the app before any portfolio feature matters. Deployment, domain, smoke-tested rebalancing.
milestone.title: Usable production deployment

items.title: Portfolio state is not reliably synced with actual Hyperliquid positions

content.body:
## Why

Raw `throw` and string-based error handling make it impossible to distinguish
between a wallet not being connected, an exchange API failure, and a network
error. Callers have to parse error messages to figure out what went wrong.

## How

Effect's typed error channels make every failure path explicit in the type
signature. Each async operation returns `Effect<A, E>` where `E` is a
discriminated union of tagged errors -- callers pattern-match on `_tag` instead
of parsing strings.

The HTTP layer defines four error types (network failure, HTTP status, JSON
parse, JSON serialize) and provides composable programs that replace raw fetch.
The Hyperliquid service wraps the existing client class, turning null-client
checks into typed `WalletNotConnected` errors and exchange API failures into
`ExchangeRequestError`. TanStack Query hooks bridge to Effect via
`Effect.runPromise` in `queryFn`, forwarding abort signals for cancellation.

Also sets up Effect tooling: eslint plugin enforcing submodule imports and the
language service for IDE support.
content.title: replace exceptions with typed Effect errors

items.title: replace exceptions with typed Effect errors

content.body:
## Why

Single-letter parameter names (`t`, `r`, `p`, `e`, `m`, `i`) hide intent and
make code harder to read at a glance. The codebase now enforces descriptive
naming for all variables and parameters.

## How

Renamed 28 single-letter variables across 12 files: arrow function parameters
(`t` -> `token`, `p` -> `position`, `r` -> `record`), loop variables (`i` ->
`index`/`dayOffset`), and local bindings (`q` -> `lowerQuery`, `r`/`g`/`b` ->
`red`/`green`/`blue`). Updated AGENTS.md to codify the rule.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
  * Internal code refactoring across multiple frontend components, utilities, and services to enhance overall code quality and long-term maintainability. Variable naming conventions throughout the codebase have been systematically improved for greater clarity and readability, making the code easier to understand and maintain. All existing functionality and user experience remain unchanged.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
content.title: rename single-letter variables to descriptive names

items.title: rename single-letter variables to descriptive names

content.body:
## Why

After the Hyperliquid Effect migration, a few async code paths still used raw
`throw`, `Promise.reject`, and untyped `fetch` -- bypassing the typed error
channel and making failures opaque to callers.

## How

Converted the remaining exception-based code to Effect programs:
`Promise.reject` in the token data query became `Effect.fail` with a tagged
`MissingTickerError`, the streaming reload mutation's `throw` became an
`EmptyStreamError`, and the beta endpoint's raw `fetch` + `throw` was replaced
with `postJson` from the Effect HTTP layer. Documented the zero-exceptions
policy in the frontend AGENTS.md with the narrow set of permitted exceptions
(context hooks, app bootstrap, imperative DOM library callbacks).

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved error handling and validation for token and stream responses, with clearer reporting for edge cases and API failures.

* **Documentation**
  * Added a new "Effect for Error Handling" section detailing error models, helpers, bridging, service wrappers, coding rules, and recommended client usage.

* **Refactor**
  * Updated client hooks to use a unified async pipeline and standardized request wrapper for more robust, maintainable data flows.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
content.title: eliminate remaining raw throws and untyped fetch calls

PR titles and descriptions were inconsistent -- some used `feat:` prefixes,

## Why

Project docs were stale — SPEC.md still referenced Privy (replaced by Turnkey),
README.md listed Python as active backend, ROADMAP.md used a flat checklist
instead of priority-ordered epics with clear motivation. Docs contradicted each
other and the actual codebase.

## How

Rewrite ROADMAP.md in epic format with WHY paragraphs, mermaid dependency
graphs, and interface-first callouts. Update SPEC.md for Privy→Turnkey and
React→SolidJS transitions. Update AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md project direction,
frontend header, and legacy Python section. Fix README.md architecture table,
add deploy commands, remove Python references. Fix frontend docs (`npx`→`bunx`,
SolidJS migration note).

* **Documentation**

  * Roadmap reorganized from phase-based to epic-centric priorities and checklists.

content.body:
## Why

Rocket is uncomposable and declining -- it doesn't integrate with the tower
middleware ecosystem that axum, apalis, and apalis-board all share. The backend
needed a tower-native HTTP layer to enable job queue monitoring and future
middleware composition.

## How

Replaced Rocket 0.5 with Axum 0.8, upgraded apalis from 0.7 to 1.0.0-rc.4,
and added apalis-board for job queue monitoring on a separate port. Handlers
converted from Rocket macros to axum extractors, shared state consolidated into
a single `AppState` struct, and e2e tests rewritten with reqwest against a real
bound listener.
content.title: migrate from Rocket to Axum with apalis-board

items.title: migrate from Rocket to Axum with apalis-board

content.body:
## Stack Context

TEE-secured wallet signing (#97, #98). The wallet crate provides a
chain-agnostic signing trait with implementations for each target chain.

## Why

The frontend currently stores raw private keys in the browser. This can't scale
to multi-venue execution or fund management. Signing needs to be decoupled from
consumers so code constrains on `W: Wallet` and works against a mock during
development while real Turnkey implementations are built in parallel.

## How

Converted the repo to a Cargo workspace and added `crates/wallet/` with a
minimal `Wallet` trait (address + sign) and a deterministic mock. One instance =
one chain + one address. Payload encoding is the caller's responsibility.
content.title: add wallet trait and mock for chain-agnostic signing

items.title: add wallet trait and mock for chain-agnostic signing

content.body:
## Stack Context

TEE-secured wallet signing (#97, #98). The wallet crate provides a
chain-agnostic signing trait with implementations for each target chain.

## Why

EVM signing is the first real implementation of the `Wallet` trait. Raw private
keys in the browser can't scale -- Turnkey runs signing inside AWS Nitro
enclaves where keys never leave attested hardware, with 50-100ms signing
latency.

## How

Added a `turnkey` feature flag to the wallet crate with an EVM implementation
that takes a pre-hashed keccak256 digest and returns an alloy Signature. The
Turnkey SDK handles key management and enclave communication. Gated behind a
feature flag to keep the dependency tree optional for consumers that only need
the trait or mock.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Integrated Turnkey support, enabling users to leverage Turnkey as an EVM wallet backend for signing transactions and managing accounts.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
content.title: add Turnkey EVM wallet implementation

content.body:
## Why

Before writing vault program code, the account model, instruction set, fee
mechanics, share arithmetic, and security mitigations need to be pinned down.
Without a spec, design decisions get made implicitly during implementation and
diverge across contributors.

## How

Added VAULT.md specifying the on-chain custody program: VaultState/Depositor
accounts, deposit/two-phase-withdraw flow, oracle-attested NAV, management +
performance fee collection via share dilution, and inflation attack prevention
via virtual shares. Updated ROADMAP.md with the vault epic broken into issues
with dependency diagrams.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Release Notes

* **Documentation**
  * Updated roadmap with comprehensive Solana vault program architecture, including deposit/withdrawal flows, share token accounting, and NAV attestation mechanics.
  * Added detailed vault program specification covering on-chain USDC custody, fee collection, and investor portfolio sharing capabilities.
  * Clarified backend-frontend integration and capital deployment workflows across multi-venue scenarios with supporting diagrams.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

content.body:
Management fee: annual percentage of depositor equity, time-accrued, collected via share dilution. Performance fee: percentage of profit above per-depositor HWM with cumulative tracking to prevent double-charging. Both split between manager and platform. Pure math -- can be developed and tested in parallel with other instruction groups. See VAULT.md for formulas.

content.body:
## Problem
When an ingest process is started and then crashes or is manually cancelled, the database does not record that the ingest run failed.

As a result, it is often necessary to fully reset/rebuild the database before a new ingest can be started, which is unsafe, inconvenient, and loses useful history.

## Desired behavior
- Each ingest run should be represented in the DB with an explicit status/state (e.g. `running`, `completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`).
- If an ingest crashes or is cancelled, the corresponding record should be updated to `failed` or `cancelled`.
- Subsequent ingests should be able to start without requiring a full DB reset, while still making it obvious in the data that a prior ingest did not complete successfully.

## Notes
- Consider how partial data from a failed ingest is represented and whether it should be safely resumable or must be discarded.
- This issue is about correct state tracking/observability in the DB, not about fixing any specific crash.
content.title: Mark failed/cancelled ingest runs in DB

milestone.description: Users need to reach the app before any portfolio feature matters. Deployment, domain, smoke-tested rebalancing.
milestone.title: Usable production deployment

items.title: Mark failed/cancelled ingest runs in DB

content.body:
## Problem
Starting an ingest run currently requires having the HTTP server running and then manually issuing a `curl` request to the ingest endpoint.

This workflow is inconvenient and error-prone:
- It requires remembering the exact endpoint and payload.
- It depends on having the server already running.
- It is hard to script or integrate into automated workflows (e.g. cron, systemd, CI) in a clean way.

## Desired behavior
- Provide a single unified command (e.g. a CLI subcommand or a dedicated binary) to trigger ingest directly, without needing to manually craft `curl` requests.
- The command should:
  - Accept the same parameters as the current ingest HTTP endpoint (or a well-designed subset/superset).
  - Return clear exit codes and logs so it can be used in automation.
  - Either:
    - start ingest end-to-end itself, **or**
    - talk to the running service in a stable, well-defined way (if the architecture requires the server).

## Notes
- Consider how this CLI entrypoint fits into the existing Nix/dev environment (e.g. `cargo run -- ingest ...` or a dedicated binary).
- The goal is to make ingest easy to trigger both manually (local dev) and in automated environments without needing ad-hoc `curl` scripts.
content.title: Add a single unified command to trigger ingest

milestone.description: Internal refactors and infra that unblock or de-risk product work: framework migrations, ingest DX, observability.
milestone.title: Backend foundations

items.title: Add a single unified command to trigger ingest

* **New Features**
  * Redesigned Portfolio rebalancer UI: positions list, per-position rows, leverage dialog, staged changes panel, alerts, and improved add/remove/weight/notional controls.
  * Precise rebalancing workflow and two-leg execution for small same-side adjustments.
  * Wallet disconnect from header and a “Page not found” route.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Safer error fallback: detailed info shown in development only, generic message in production.
  * Pointer-event utility to allow disabled containers while keeping actionable controls interactive.

* **Documentation**
  * Added documentation describing the portfolio rebalancer execution model and action payloads.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
content.title: Fix/placing order and rendering logic

items.title: Fix/placing order and rendering logic

content.body:
## Context
`frontend/src/pages/Portfolio/components/PositionsPanel/LeverageDialog.tsx` is currently a raw leverage editor:
- It opens via a small `ghost` button and renders an inline modal.
- The slider immediately calls `props.onLeverageChange(props.symbol, leverage)` on every change.
- The only keyboard handling is a `window` `keydown` listener that closes on `Escape`.
- There is no explicit Apply/Cancel workflow, focus management, or accessibility semantics (dialog role/aria, focus trap, initial focus, keyboard support beyond Escape).

## Goal
Design and refactor `LeverageDialog` into a polished, production-ready component with the intended UX and better correctness/safety.

## Requirements (English)
1. **New UI design**
   - Match the app's existing UI patterns (buttons, dialog layout, typography, spacing).
   - Show current leverage, max leverage, and the user-selected leverage.
   - Display a clear affordance for committing or discarding changes.

2. **Apply / Cancel behavior**
   - Slider/input should update a local draft value.
   - `props.onLeverageChange` must be called only when the user clicks **Apply** (or equivalent).
   - **Cancel / Close** should revert the draft and close the dialog.

3. **Validation and clamping**
   - Draft leverage must always stay within `[min, maxLeverage]`.
   - Ensure consistent rounding/step behavior (avoid unexpected values).

4. **Accessibility & keyboard UX**
   - Add proper dialog semantics (`role="dialog"`, `aria-modal`, accessible title/description).
   - Implement focus trapping within the dialog.
   - Set initial focus when the dialog opens (e.g., slider handle or first action button).
   - Ensure keyboard navigation works naturally (Tab/Shift-Tab).
   - Keep the current `Escape`-to-close behavior.

5. **Disabled state correctness**
   - If `props.disabled` is true, the dialog must not open and no interactions should be possible.

6. **Behavioral consistency**
   - Make sure backdrop click and close actions behave consistently.
   - Preserve the existing intent of the `createEffect` `window` listener for Escape (do not refactor it into derived state/event sources incorrectly).

## Notes / Constraints
- The component currently uses an imperative `createEffect` with a `window` `keydown` listener for `Escape` and removes it via `onCleanup`.
- This refactor should improve the UX while keeping correct lifecycle management for that listener.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No `TODO`/placeholder UI remains in LeverageDialog.
- The dialog has Apply/Cancel and does not mutate leverage until Apply.
- Accessibility checks pass (focus trap + dialog semantics).
- Lint/typecheck pass.
content.title: Refactor LeverageDialog: new design + apply/cancel + accessibility

milestone.description: Users need to reach the app before any portfolio feature matters. Deployment, domain, smoke-tested rebalancing.
milestone.title: Usable production deployment

items.title: Refactor LeverageDialog: new design + apply/cancel + accessibility

content.body:
## Context
useBeta (frontend/src/pages/Portfolio/hooks/useBeta.ts) computes normalized weights by deriving totalNotional on the client:
- weightsFromPortfolio(portfolio) does Object.values(portfolio).reduce((sum, position) => sum + position.notional, 0)
- then computes weights as position.notional / totalNotional

However, usePortfolioState (frontend/src/pages/Portfolio/hooks/usePortfolioState.ts) already calculates targetTotalNotional (and currentTotalNotional).

## Problem
We currently duplicate work (client-side totalNotional recomputation), even though the same value already exists in state.
This increases CPU work and can also trigger unnecessary query invalidations/refetches when the weights object identity changes.

## Goal
Refactor useBeta to reuse the already computed notional totals from usePortfolioState, making the beta request more efficient.

## Suggested approach
1. Update useBeta API so the caller can provide totalNotional (most likely targetTotalNotional, since index.tsx passes portfolio.targetPortfolio).
2. In weightsFromPortfolio, avoid recomputing totalNotional; instead compute weights as position.notional / providedTotalNotional.
3. Keep filtering out undefined portfolio slots before reading notional/symbol.
4. Consider tightening queryKey to depend on stable primitives (instead of the full weights object reference) to reduce unnecessary refetches.

## Acceptance criteria
- useBeta no longer recomputes totalNotional via a reduce over the entire portfolio.
- Types align with usePortfolioState: Record<string, PortfolioInterface | undefined>.
- Typecheck/lint pass.
- Beta query refetches only when inputs that affect beta actually change.
content.title: Review useBeta: optimize beta requests by reusing totalNotional

milestone.description: Portfolio-weighted Bitcoin beta plus read-only wallet inclusion so the risk view reflects actual exposure.
milestone.title: Full Bitcoin beta accounting

items.title: Review useBeta: optimize beta requests by reusing totalNotional

content.body:
## Context

We want to know when a rebalance has effectively finished **without** reloading the page and **without** only hoping a fixed delay is long enough (blind wait on a "refreshing" state).

## Directions to explore

- **Polling / fetch**: explicit checks against backend or exchange state until terminal conditions are met.
- **Subscriptions**: a push-style signal (there may be a non-obvious subscription path worth investigating later, including SSR-related patterns).

## Partial failures

If **part** of a multi-step rebalance fails (e.g. during an early "close everything" phase), we need a product decision:

- Example: something sold but a leg did not complete as planned — should we **stop** further rebalancing steps that depend on that outcome, while still allowing unrelated follow-up buys that are not tied to the failed token?
- Document trade-offs and a recommended default.

## Future knob (low priority)

- A **"fail fast"** toggle: abort the rest of the plan as soon as any step fails, vs. best-effort continuation. Likely low priority for now; capture for later design.

## Acceptance (when implemented)

- [ ] Document chosen completion criteria per execution mode (what counts as "done").
- [ ] UX: user-visible terminal state without mandatory full page reload.
- [ ] Document behavior on partial failure (stop / continue / user prompt).

/cc discussion 2026-03-31 (Gleb Dianov, Lev Shatrov)
content.title: Rebalance: detect completion without reload; handle partial failures (fail-fast later)

milestone.description: Users need to reach the app before any portfolio feature matters. Deployment, domain, smoke-tested rebalancing.
milestone.title: Usable production deployment

items.title: Rebalance: detect completion without reload; handle partial failures (fail-fast later)

content.body:
## Context

Toggling between testnet and mainnet from the wallet header forces a full page reload via `window.location.reload()` in `useSwitchNetwork` (`frontend/src/hooks/useTrading.ts`).

The reload is currently used to re-initialize all in-memory state and local storage-backed portfolio snapshots for the new network. This is jarring UX (loses scroll position, dialog state, in-flight edits, dev console state) and pairs with a TODO at the top of `frontend/src/components/wallet-header.tsx`.

## Problem

- A network switch should not require a full document reload.
- All network-scoped state (queries, portfolio snapshots, local storage entries, websocket subscriptions) needs to be invalidated/reset coherently when the active network changes, without dropping the page.
- Today the reload masks gaps in invalidation — without it, stale testnet data would bleed into mainnet views (and vice versa).
content.title: Network switch (testnet/mainnet) reloads the page

milestone.description: Users need to reach the app before any portfolio feature matters. Deployment, domain, smoke-tested rebalancing.
milestone.title: Usable production deployment

items.title: Network switch (testnet/mainnet) reloads the page

User story: [Authenticate Portfolio Ownership By Solana Pubkey](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x00f.authenticate-portfolio-ownership-by-solana-pubkey.md)

Scope: persist the portfolio metadata schema, keyed by Solana pubkey, with read/write endpoints that enforce session ownership. Covers the ACs about durable storage of portfolio state.
content.title: feat: backend persistence for portfolio metadata

milestone.description: Solana pubkey as portfolio identity, shareable URLs, optional paid privacy.
milestone.title: Portfolio identity and sharing

Scope: frontend loads portfolio metadata on sign-in and syncs writes back to the backend over an authenticated session. Covers the ACs about end-to-end portfolio identity in the UI.

content.body:
User story: [Roll Protective Puts Before Final Month](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x016.roll-protective-puts-before-final-month.md)

Scope: define and persist a put ladder. Supports moneyness rungs (5/10/20/30% OTM), delta targets (0.10/0.25/0.40), and tenor durations mapped to nearest listed expiry. Defaults: 10/20/30% OTM at 30/90/180 days. Covers the AC about ladder definition.
content.title: feat: put ladder definition with moneyness, delta, and tenor parameters

milestone.description: Protective puts (manual + Derive), historical and stressed crash simulations, automated ladder rolls.
milestone.title: Crash protection and simulation

items.title: feat: put ladder definition with moneyness, delta, and tenor parameters

content.body:
User story: [Roll Protective Puts Before Final Month](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x016.roll-protective-puts-before-final-month.md)

Scope: implement the selection function (e.g. `identifyPutsToRoll`) that reads `rollThresholdDays` from the ladder config (default 30) and returns puts whose remaining days to expiry are below the threshold. Validates the parameter at ladder creation. Covers the AC about which puts get rolled.
content.title: feat: identify puts to roll using rollThresholdDays

milestone.description: Protective puts (manual + Derive), historical and stressed crash simulations, automated ladder rolls.
milestone.title: Crash protection and simulation

items.title: feat: identify puts to roll using rollThresholdDays

content.body:
User story: [Roll Protective Puts Before Final Month](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x016.roll-protective-puts-before-final-month.md)

Scope: implement the deterministic selection algorithm that stages the sell + buy legs of each roll. Covers strike rules (preserve moneyness or delta), expiry rules (nearest listed, tie-break by longer expiry then OI), quantity rules (delta-preserving or notional-preserving), partial-fill handling, and contract-lot rounding (round up to preserve coverage).
content.title: feat: deterministic put roll staging (strike, expiry, quantity selection)

milestone.description: Protective puts (manual + Derive), historical and stressed crash simulations, automated ladder rolls.
milestone.title: Crash protection and simulation

items.title: feat: deterministic put roll staging (strike, expiry, quantity selection)

content.body:
User story: [Roll Protective Puts Before Final Month](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x016.roll-protective-puts-before-final-month.md)

Scope: implement the `coverageRatio = sum(put_delta * put_quantity) / underlying_exposure` validation, with threshold resolution per-strategy > per-ladder > global `hedgeContinuityMin` (default 0.80). Failure response includes both the computed `coverageRatio` and the required threshold plus source.
content.title: feat: hedge continuity validation for put rolls

milestone.description: Protective puts (manual + Derive), historical and stressed crash simulations, automated ladder rolls.
milestone.title: Crash protection and simulation

items.title: feat: hedge continuity validation for put rolls

content.body:
User story: [Roll Protective Puts Before Final Month](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x016.roll-protective-puts-before-final-month.md)

Scope: the roll review step shows the net debit/credit across all legs, premium delta per leg, and a warning when the net debit exceeds a configurable threshold (default 1.5x the prior roll). Surfaces IV spikes and liquidity gaps before execution.
content.title: feat: put roll cost transparency in review step

milestone.description: Protective puts (manual + Derive), historical and stressed crash simulations, automated ladder rolls.
milestone.title: Crash protection and simulation

items.title: feat: put roll cost transparency in review step

User story: [Use Derive Options For Protective Puts](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x019.use-derive-options-for-protective-puts.md)

Scope: option chain UI with paging (50 contracts/page), sortable by expiry/strike, filterable by expiry/strike/IV/volume. Greeks shown in detail view. BTC spot price visible in header.
content.title: feat: derive option chain browser

Scope: execute a put option purchase on Derive through the user's connected wallet (non-custodial). Includes pre-trade preview (cost, premium, fees) and post-trade confirmation.

content.body:
User story: [Use Derive Options For Protective Puts](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x019.use-derive-options-for-protective-puts.md)

Scope: surface Derive option positions in the portfolio view as a distinct section from spot/perps. Includes contract, expiry, strike, quantity, current value, and PnL.

items.title: feat: derive option positions in portfolio view

content.body:
User story: [Use Derive Options For Protective Puts](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x019.use-derive-options-for-protective-puts.md)

Scope: value Derive option positions using current mark; mark older than 60s is flagged stale. Fallback order: mark > mid > last trade > unavailable. Positions flow through the risk view alongside manually-entered puts.
content.title: feat: derive option valuation with staleness warning and fallback

milestone.description: Protective puts (manual + Derive), historical and stressed crash simulations, automated ladder rolls.
milestone.title: Crash protection and simulation

items.title: feat: derive option valuation with staleness warning and fallback

content.body:
User story: [Show Risk Analytics For Active Portfolio](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x01b.show-risk-analytics-for-active-portfolio.md)

Scope: compute and display Conditional VaR (expected shortfall) at the same configurable confidence levels as VaR, using the shared measurement-window contract and weighting.
content.title: feat: CVaR at configurable confidence levels

milestone.description: Portfolio risk assessment beyond beta: VaR, CVaR, drawdown, correlation, ENB, Monte Carlo.
milestone.title: Risk analytics

items.title: feat: CVaR at configurable confidence levels

content.body:

Scope: compute and display historical maximum drawdown and drawdown duration over the active measurement window, with a visualization of the drawdown curve.
content.title: feat: historical drawdown analysis

content.body:
User story: [Show Risk Analytics For Active Portfolio](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x01b.show-risk-analytics-for-active-portfolio.md)

Scope: compute the effective number of independent bets (ENB) from the correlation matrix and position weights -- a single scalar that summarizes diversification. Surfaces alongside the matrix view.
content.title: feat: effective number of bets accounting for correlations

milestone.description: Portfolio risk assessment beyond beta: VaR, CVaR, drawdown, correlation, ENB, Monte Carlo.

content.body:
User story: [Show Risk Analytics For Active Portfolio](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/stories/0x01b.show-risk-analytics-for-active-portfolio.md)

Scope: run a Monte Carlo simulation over the active portfolio at a user-selected horizon, with configurable simulation count. Returns percentile bands for portfolio value and shows them in the risk panel.
content.title: feat: monte carlo simulation with horizon selection
